methods.description: This method describes the process of evaluating pollen performance in field populations of Clarkia sister taxa by hand-pollinating flowers and measuring pollen tube growth.

steps.description: In spring 2008, we evaluated pollen performance in a total of eight field populations (2 per taxon) near Lake Isabella (Kern and Tulare Counties, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: collect field data on pollen performance in Clarkia populations

steps.description: In each population, we haphazardly sampled 18-22 individuals to serve as pollen donors; using their pollen, we evaluated pollen performance following hand-pollination of recipient flowers on other individuals in the same population.

To ensure that each recipient flower was free of pollen before administering hand pollinations, unguiculata and xantiana flowers were emasculated (the anther sacs were removed) after they opened, but before stigmas became receptive.

Following emasculation, in all four focal taxa, 1-2 days prior to the hand-pollination of the recipients' flowers, we covered the pre-receptive, emasculated recipient flowers or buds with a an approximately 5 cm segment of a hollow, partially transparent, perforated plastic cylinders (10 mm in diameter, -boba‖ straws) to prevent self-pollination and pollen deposition by visiting insects.
steps.searchable_summary: cover flowers with perforated cylinders to prevent insect pollination

steps.description: The cylinders were removed immediately prior to hand-pollinations, which were performed on receptive, virgin stigmas by applying a single, clump-free, layer of fresh pollen to receptive floral stigmas with a blunted dissecting probe.
Following hand-pollination, cylinders were placed on each flower to prevent additional pollen deposition by insects.
steps.searchable_summary: replace cylinders to prevent further pollen deposition

steps.description: We recorded air temperature next to each flower at the time of pollination and at the time of style harvest (°C) with a digital thermometer.
steps.searchable_summary: record air temperature during pollination and style harvest

steps.description: After allowing pollen tubes to grow for exactly 2.5 hours, whole flowers were excised from the plant and fixed in formalin-acetic acid (FAA) to arrest pollen tube growth.
steps.searchable_summary: excise and fix flowers to stop pollen tube growth

steps.description: Flowers were then brought to the laboratory for style excision and pollen tube visualization.
steps.searchable_summary: transport flowers to lab for style excision and visualization
